domain: identity & multi-tenant (auth, users, permissions, orgs, branches)

operations.summary: Get one branch incl printer config

keySchemas.name: LoginRequest
keySchemas.quirks: All fields nullable (dual PIN/email mode); pin pattern ^[0-9]{4,6}$. Generator emits everything Option<T>; client must enforce mode invariants manually.

keySchemas.name: LoginResponse / MeResponse
keySchemas.quirks: tax_rate is a JSON number (double), NOT a BigDecimal string here despite being BigDecimal in DB. currency_code/tax_rate required. Cache MeResponse for offline session restore.

keySchemas.name: UserPublic
keySchemas.quirks: org_id, branch_id, email, phone all nullable. role is UserRole enum with no unknown-default — a new server role breaks deserialization; wrap with serde other/default.

keySchemas.name: UserRole
keySchemas.quirks: String enum (super_admin/org_admin/branch_manager/teller), no unknown variant. PrinterBrand (star/epson) and Action enums share this no-default risk.

keySchemas.name: Branch
keySchemas.quirks: latitude/longitude/printer_* all nullable; printer_brand uses oneOf[null, PrinterBrand] wrapper (generator may produce awkward Option). timezone always present (resolved server-side).

keySchemas.name: UpdateBranchRequest / UpdateUserRequest / UpdateOrgRequest
keySchemas.quirks: PATCH-style: every field optional+nullable. OpenAPI cannot express absent-vs-null; null on printer_* means CLEAR. Naive generated client sends nulls and wipes columns — must serialize skip-if-none.

keySchemas.name: Org
keySchemas.quirks: tax_rate documented as BigDecimal internally but transmitted as JSON number; logo_url/receipt_footer nullable. CreateOrgMultipart is multipart/form-data with binary logo — generated client often mishandles multipart.

keySchemas.name: Permission / RolePermission / UserPermissionItem / PermissionMatrix
keySchemas.quirks: resource and action are free-form strings (no enum) — cache as opaque strings. PermissionMatrix.role_default/user_override nullable booleans (tri-state); effective is the resolved value to gate UI.

notes: Domain is identity/multi-tenant: almost entirely back-office. Only the auth handshake plus identity reads are on the teller path. STRATEGY: (1) auth/login and resolve-branch are online-only — a teller cannot start a brand-new session offline (login validates PIN server-side, geofence needs GPS+network); cache the JWT/session locally so an already-logged-in teller survives days offline without re-hitting login. (2) me, permissions, the user's own branch, get_org, and timezones are read-cache: mirror them at login into SQLite so the POS can render identity, tax_rate, currency, printer config, and permission gating fully offline; refresh on reconnect via delta sync. (3) Everything user/permission/org admin and branch CRUD is online-only — not the selling hot-path. NO outbox-mutation operations exist in this domain: there is no write a teller performs during normal selling here (orders/payments/shift live in other tags). needsIdempotency=true set on non-idempotent admin POSTs (create_org/user/branch, assign_branch, complete_onboarding) so retries after a flaky reconnect don't duplicate — even though they run online-only. SCHEMA-QUIRK SUMMARY for the Rust generator: the classic BigDecimal-as-JSON-string quirk does NOT appear in this domain — tax_rate is a real JSON number (double); the string-decimal trap lives in inventory/pricing schemas, not here. The quirks that WILL bite: (a) PATCH update requests where absent-vs-null is collapsed — generated clients must skip_serializing_if None or they will null out printer_ip/role/logo; (b) oneOf[{null}, $ref] enum wrappers (printer_brand, role on UpdateUserRequest) that produce clumsy nested Option; (c) enums with no unknown/default variant (UserRole, PrinterBrand) that panic on a future server value — add serde(other); (d) pervasive nullable scalars (org_id/branch_id/email/phone/lat/long) all Option<T>; (e) permission resource/action are untyped strings, intentionally open-ended. Also note CreateOrgMultipart and upload_org_logo are multipart/form-data with binary fields, which the openapi-generator frequently emits incorrectly — verify those by hand.

domain: menu/catalog & pricing (menu, categories, addon-items, bundles, discounts, payment_methods, branch overrides)

keySchemas.name: MenuItem / MenuItemFull
keySchemas.quirks: base_price is integer int32 minor-units (NOT BigDecimal string). MenuItemFull is allOf[MenuItem + inline] — generators flatten poorly. name/description_translations are untyped object → opaque map/Value. Many ['string','null'] fields (category_id, image_url, deleted_at).

keySchemas.name: ItemSize / UpsertSizeRequest / BranchSizeOverride
keySchemas.quirks: price_override is integer int32 minor-units. Size identity is by label string in branch/override contexts — no uuid, easy to mis-key locally.

keySchemas.name: Discount / CreateDiscountRequest
keySchemas.quirks: dtype is plain string with NO enum constraint (percent/fixed open-ended) — model as String, do not assume variants. value is integer int32 (cents OR basis-points depending on dtype — ambiguous, verify). name_translations untyped object.

keySchemas.name: AddonItem / AddonCost / AddonIngredient
keySchemas.quirks: default_price/price integer (int32 on item, int64 on cost rows — mixed widths). addon_type plain string, no enum. cost is ['integer','null'] int64. quantity_used is number/double (recipe), not a string — but watch f64 precision. primary_ingredient_id nullable uuid.

keySchemas.name: Bundle / BundleWithComponents / BundleStatus
keySchemas.quirks: status enum [draft,active,archived] has NO default — unknown variant will fail deserialization; add serde unknown-default. price integer int32. available_from/until_time are ['string','null'] with no time format (won't map to chrono NaiveTime). available_*_date use fmt=date. description_translations schema is {} (any-JSON). BundleWithComponents is allOf composition.

keySchemas.name: BranchMenuOverride / BranchAddonOverride
keySchemas.quirks: PUT/DELETE keyed by branch_id+item/addon_id in BODY (no path id) — non-RESTful, careful with client method shape. price_override ['integer','null'] int32. is_available boolean drives availability — must merge over base menu in local mirror.

keySchemas.name: OrgPaymentMethod
keySchemas.quirks: label_translations untyped/None schema (any-JSON). is_cash + is_active booleans gate tender UI. color/icon required strings. Safe to mirror read-only for offline tender.

keySchemas.name: Category
keySchemas.quirks: name_translations untyped object; image_url & deleted_at nullable; soft-delete via deleted_at means cache must filter, not just upsert.

notes: Spec is OpenAPI 3.1.0 (utoipa). IMPORTANT correction to the brief's assumption: in THIS domain money is NOT serialized as BigDecimal JSON strings — all prices/costs are integer minor-units (int32 on catalog rows, int64 on costing rows). The string-decimal quirk (quantity_used/current_stock/reorder_threshold) lives in the inventory/stock domain, NOT here; the only float here is recipe quantity_used as number/double. Generator pitfalls that WILL bite the Rust client: (1) enums BundleStatus and implicit dtype/addon_type — BundleStatus is a closed enum with no default (add #[serde(other)] fallback); dtype/addon_type are open plain strings (do not generate enums). (2) Heavy ['T','null'] nullability under 3.1 — ensure Option<T> generation, not skipped fields. (3) allOf composition (MenuItemFull, MenuItemWithCosts, BundleWithComponents) flattens unevenly. (4) *_translations are untyped object / empty schema → map to serde_json::Value or HashMap<String,String>. (5) available_from/until_time are bare strings (no format) — keep as String, don't expect NaiveTime. Offline strategy: this is the read hot-path — every GET (menu, categories, addons, bundles/available, discounts, payment-methods, branch overrides) must mirror to SQLite and refresh via delta sync. NO outbox-mutation in this domain: all writes are back-office catalog/pricing edits that are acceptably online-only (the selling outbox lives in orders/payments/shifts). Branch overrides must be layered over base catalog when building the local sellable menu. Reports (bundle_performance, costing/catalog) are online-only.

operations.summary: Place sale; THE selling hot path
Ingredient preview; rare, not hot path
Send OTP; customer app, requires net

keySchemas.name: CreateOrderRequest
keySchemas.quirks: Outbox payload. Money is int32 minor-units (subtotal/tax_amount/total/discount_amount/amount_tendered/change_given), NOT BigDecimal strings. Many nullable int32 fields. Accepts client-supplied created_at (date-time, nullable) — set it for offline replay. branch_id+shift_id+items required; no Idempotency-Key header server-side, client must add one.

keySchemas.name: OrderFull
keySchemas.quirks: allOf(Order) + items + nullable delivery (oneOf null|OrderDeliveryInfo via utoipa oneOf-with-null pattern, trips many generators) + warnings[] (oversell flags). Reconcile server id/order_number against client temp-id after replay.

keySchemas.name: Order
keySchemas.quirks: All money int32 minor-units. order_type/payment_method/status are free-form strings (no enum) — treat as unknown-default string, do not generate enums. Heavy nullable: void_*, tip_*, discount_id, delivery_* coords (double, nullable).

keySchemas.name: OrderItemInput
keySchemas.quirks: Selling line item. quantity int32, unit_price nullable int32 minor-units. Polymorphic-ish: bundle_id OR menu_item_id (both nullable). addons/optional_field_ids/bundle_components are untyped arrays (items schema omitted) — generator emits Vec<Value>/Vec<serde_json::Value>.

keySchemas.name: DeliveryOrderInput
keySchemas.quirks: Customer-app (public) create, online-only. customer_lat/lng are double nullable. channel/payment_method_hint free-form strings (no enum). device_token required (from otp_verify). items are CartLineInput, not OrderItemInput.

keySchemas.name: StatusInput
keySchemas.quirks: status is a free-form string documented as confirmed|preparing|ready|out_for_delivery but NOT an OpenAPI enum — must handle as unknown-default string. Side-effecting (stamps steps, may fire one WhatsApp) so replay must be idempotent.

keySchemas.name: VoidOrderRequest
keySchemas.quirks: reason free-form string; note required only when reason=='other' (cross-field rule not in schema). restore_inventory nullable bool. voided_at nullable date-time — set client-side for offline.

keySchemas.name: DeliveryZone / QuoteResponse
keySchemas.quirks: fee and max_road_distance_meters/distance_meters are int32 minor-units/meters (NOT BigDecimal strings). name_translations is an untyped object (generates HashMap<String,Value>). QuoteResponse.status free-form string ok|out_of_range|unavailable, no enum.

notes: Money in this domain is int32 minor-units (piasters), NOT the BigDecimal-as-string quirk — that lives in inventory/recipe schemas (e.g. quantity_used/current_stock). The only float here: customer_lat/lng (double, nullable) and PreviewIngredient.quantity (double). Watch-outs for the Rust generator: (1) utoipa nullable pattern is type:[T,"null"] and OrderFull.delivery uses oneOf:[{type:null},{$ref}] — both need custom Option handling, many generators mis-emit. (2) status/order_type/payment_method/channel/reason are free-form strings documented with a value set but no enum — generate as String with unknown-default, never as Rust enums. (3) untyped arrays (addons, optional_field_ids, bundle_components) and untyped objects (name_translations) become serde_json::Value. Outbox set (replay in order, temp-id reconciliation): create_order, void_order, set_status, set_prep_time, finalize_delivery_order, cancel_delivery_order. No server-side Idempotency-Key header exists on any create endpoint — client owns the key. set_status/finalize have realtime side effects (WhatsApp, customer tracking) so offline replay can fire stale notifications; consider suppress-on-late-replay. All /public/* and /otp/* are the customer delivery web app, not the teller, hence online-only. Spec source: /Users/shawket/Desktop/MadarRust/openapi.json

operations.summary: Open shift; client sends id + opened_at
SKU recipe costs; admin costing view

keySchemas.name: OpenShiftRequest
keySchemas.quirks: Client-supplied nullable id(uuid) + opened_at for offline reconcile; opening_cash int32; opening_cash_edited documented as IGNORED by server (derived); needs idempotency key.

keySchemas.name: CloseShiftRequest
keySchemas.quirks: closing_cash_declared int32 (money in piastres); nullable closed_at lets POS stamp real offline time; cash_note nullable.

keySchemas.name: CashMovementRequest
keySchemas.quirks: amount int32; nullable created_at — POS sends real time for offline movements, server stamps now() when omitted; future values rejected.

keySchemas.name: Shift
keySchemas.quirks: Money fields int32 (opening/closing/discrepancy) — MISMATCH with int64 used in ShiftSummary; status is bare string with NO enum (unknown-default not modeled); ~12 nullable [T,null] fields; branch_name only set in list view.

keySchemas.name: CashMovement
keySchemas.quirks: Money amount int32 vs CashMovementSummaryRow also int32 but ShiftReport aggregates int64; all uuid/date-time fields required.

keySchemas.name: ShiftSummary
keySchemas.quirks: All money int64 (vs Shift int32); revenue_by_method is an EMPTY schema {} -> generates untyped serde_json::Value / `any`; several nullable closing fields.

keySchemas.name: ShiftReportResponse
keySchemas.quirks: Z-report; all aggregates int64 piastres; embeds Shift (int32 mix); expected_cash authoritative — display directly, do not re-derive.

keySchemas.name: SkuCost
keySchemas.quirks: price int64 piastres (NOT BigDecimal-string); cost nullable int64 with cost_missing bool sentinel; food_cost_pct/margin_pct nullable double; size_label 'one_size' magic value.

keySchemas.name: LowStockRow
keySchemas.quirks: current_stock/reorder_threshold/deficit are number/double (NOT JSON strings here); supplier_id/supplier_name nullable; unit is free string.

keySchemas.name: ValuationRow
keySchemas.quirks: current_stock number/double; cost_per_unit & value nullable int64 piastres (null when cost unknown) — null is meaningful, not zero.

notes: All operations are GET except 5 shift writes: open/close/cash-movement (outbox-mutation, the teller selling hot-path) and force-close/delete (online-only back-office, manager authority). Spec exposes EXACTLY the offline-first hooks expected: open/close/cash-movement requests accept a client-supplied uuid `id` and real-event timestamps (opened_at/closed_at/created_at), explicitly documented for movements made OFFLINE so they keep their true time and reconcile after sync — this IS the temp-id/idempotency contract. The classic BigDecimal-as-string quirk does NOT bite this domain: all money is integer piastres (int32 on Shift/CashMovement, int64 on summaries/reports/costing) and stock quantities are number/double — no decimals-as-strings here (those live in inventory/purchasing schemas). The real Rust-client traps: (1) int32-vs-int64 split on the same logical money fields between Shift and ShiftSummary/reports; (2) `status` and `payment_method`/`addon_type` are bare strings with no enum and no unknown-default modeled; (3) ShiftSummary.revenue_by_method is an empty `{}` schema -> generator emits serde_json::Value; (4) pervasive nullable [T,\"null\"] optionals where null is semantically distinct from zero (e.g. cost null = unknown vs cost_missing bool); (5) opening_cash_edited is documented as server-ignored — don't trust it round-tripping. Mirror shifts + cash-movements + shift report/summary locally (read-cache) so the teller can print Z-reports offline; keep all analytical/org reports and costing online-only.

domain: inventory & back-office (inventory, purchasing, stocktakes, recipes, menu_advisor)

operations.summary: Movement ledger; large, rarely needed offline
Admin adds ingredient to branch stock

keySchemas.name: BranchInventoryItem
keySchemas.quirks: Read mirror for branch stock. current_stock/reorder_threshold are number/double (NOT BigDecimal strings here). Many nullable doubles: cost_per_unit, par_min, par_max, last_counted_at. cost_per_unit is piastres-per-unit but typed double; null means unknown, not free.

keySchemas.name: OrgIngredient
keySchemas.quirks: Catalog read mirror. cost_per_unit nullable double (piastres, null=unknown). pack_size/density_g_per_ml/yield_pct all nullable doubles; null != 0. Generator must map null to Option, not default 0.0.

keySchemas.name: BranchInventoryMovement
keySchemas.quirks: movement_type is a free string (sale|waste|transfer_in|purchase_in|stock_count...), NOT an enum schema — no compile-time variants; treat as open string. quantity is signed double. Most fields nullable.

keySchemas.name: PurchaseOrderLine
keySchemas.quirks: Money split: unit_cost is integer/int64 piastres, while quantity_ordered/quantity_received/units_per_purchase_unit are number/double. Easy to swap int vs float in generated client.

keySchemas.name: PurchaseOrderFull / StocktakeFull
keySchemas.quirks: allOf composition (base + extra props). Some Rust openapi-generators flatten allOf badly or emit a wrapper struct; verify field flattening. StocktakeFull adds required variance_threshold_pct double.

keySchemas.name: StocktakeItem
keySchemas.quirks: counted_qty/variance nullable doubles; unit_cost nullable int64 piastres; variance_reason is free-string enum (theft|spoilage|...) typed as plain nullable string — no enum schema.

keySchemas.name: PriceSuggestion
keySchemas.quirks: Money fields current_price/effective_price are int64 piastres; cm_per_unit and what-if margins are nullable doubles. References nested enum schemas Action/Classification/Confidence which ARE closed enums.

keySchemas.name: RunStatus / SuggestionKind / Decision
keySchemas.quirks: Closed string enums with NO unknown/default variant (in_progress|completed|failed; price|bundle|removal; accepted|rejected|ignored). A generated Rust enum will hard-error if the backend adds a variant — add #[serde(other)] fallback. Note RecordDecisionBody.decision is deliberately a plain string (server validates -> 400) while list responses use the closed enum: asymmetric.

notes: Whole domain is back-office: nothing here is on the selling hot path. Only one outbox-mutation: POST .../waste (create_waste) — tellers log spoilage/breakage mid-shift offline, so it needs a durable outbox + idempotency key + client temp-id. Everything else mutating is admin/manager (catalog, transfers, suppliers, POs, stocktakes, recipes, advisor) = online-only, acceptable to require connectivity. read-cache reads worth mirroring locally for the inventory home screen: list_branch_stock, list_catalog, get_inventory_settings, and recipe lists (list_drink_recipes/list_addon_ingredients) since recipes drive stock-depletion math the POS may apply locally on each sale. list_movements, all menu-advisor reads, variance reports and PO detail are too large/rare to mirror -> online-only. QUIRK SUMMARY confirmed against the spec: the feared BigDecimal-serialized-as-JSON-STRING pattern does NOT appear in this domain — quantity_used, current_stock, reorder_threshold, quantity_ordered are all number/double, and money is integer/int64 piastres (cents-equivalent), never strings. So the real generator risks are: (1) the int64-piastres vs double-quantity split on the same struct (PurchaseOrderLine, StocktakeItem); (2) pervasive nullable scalars where null means 'unknown' not 0/free (cost_per_unit etc.) — must be Option<T>; (3) closed enums (RunStatus/SuggestionKind/Decision plus nested Action/Classification/Confidence) lacking an unknown-default — add a serde catch-all so a newer backend doesn't break deserialization; (4) movement_type and variance_reason are free strings, not enums — don't assume a fixed variant set; (5) allOf composition on PurchaseOrderFull/StocktakeFull needs verifying after generation.

domain: integrations & misc (whatsapp, uploads, qr, timezones, public)

keySchemas.name: QrResponse
keySchemas.quirks: Freeform string 'kind' enum (no variants); qr_data_url is a huge base64 data: URI — do not mirror. Returned by all QR GETs and create_marketing_link.

keySchemas.name: WhatsappStatus
keySchemas.quirks: 7 required bools + nullable paused_at(date-time) and qr_image; qr_image is a base64 data: URL. Live gateway snapshot, never cache.

keySchemas.name: MarketingLink
keySchemas.quirks: nullable 'label'; freeform string 'kind' enum; date-time created_at. UUID id.

keySchemas.name: BranchTable
keySchemas.quirks: All UUIDs + date-time created_at/updated_at; clean and cacheable. Only solid read-cache schema in domain.

keySchemas.name: UploadImageMultipart
keySchemas.quirks: multipart/form-data with format:binary 'image' (contentMediaType image/*) — generators handle file fields poorly; UploadResponse is just {image_url}.

keySchemas.name: DeliveryMenuItem / DeliveryMenuDiscount / DeliveryAddonOption
keySchemas.quirks: Money is integer int32 piastres (NOT BigDecimal-string here); 'name_translations' is an untyped open object (type:object, no shape) -> generates as Value/Map; nullable category_id/default_milk_addon_id; freeform 'dtype' enum.

keySchemas.name: QuoteResponse
keySchemas.quirks: All-nullable fee/distance_meters/zone_* with status as freeform string ('ok'|'out_of_range'|'unavailable') — model status as unknown-default enum.

keySchemas.name: DeliveryOrderInput
keySchemas.quirks: Many nullable address fields; freeform 'channel' and 'payment_method_hint' string enums; double lat/lng nullable; requires device_token from OTP verify.

notes: Scope note: tags 'timezones' and 'public' are not literal tag names in the spec — /timezones is tagged 'branches', /public/* are tagged 'delivery-public'/'orgs'. I included them by path-prefix since they match the domain theme. Criticality: backoffice — NOTHING in this domain is on the teller selling hot-path, so there are zero outbox-mutation operations. whatsapp/uploads/qr/tables CRUD are all admin (online-only); QR GETs return giant base64 PNGs and are rare (online-only). public/* is the GUEST delivery surface (OTP, public menu, guest order placement), not the teller's own POS writes, so per the conservative rule they stay online-only — the teller consumes those orders through other endpoints. Only read-cache candidates: GET /timezones (static IANA list) and GET /branches/{id}/tables (small, stable, needed for dine-in table labels offline). Idempotency: the backend models NO Idempotency-Key header on any POST in this domain — needsIdempotency is flagged on non-idempotent creates (create_table, create_marketing_link, upload image, whatsapp pair/logout, create_delivery_order) but the client must generate keys itself; there is no server contract for them yet. Key generator gotcha: money in this domain is integer piastres (int32), so the BigDecimal-as-JSON-string trap does NOT apply here — the real traps are (1) freeform string enums everywhere (kind, dtype, channel, payment_method_hint, quote status) needing #[serde(other)] unknown-defaults, (2) untyped 'name_translations' open objects, (3) pervasive nullable Option fields, and (4) base64 data: URLs in qr_data_url/qr_image that must not be persisted.
